summary_one_line: Input must drive an entity-reference name whose first byte is 'a' (e.g. & / ') into xmlGetPredefinedEntity's switch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A parsed entity-reference name (passed as `name` to xmlGetPredefinedEntity) whose first byte name[0] equals 'a' (0x61), so the switch(name[0]) selects `case 'a':`; concretely the predefined entity names & or ' reaching the function via xmlSAX2GetEntity.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner repeatedly reaches and resolves the 'a'-case: it takes the true side 63 times (W T=63 F=0 at line 307) and the enclosing function shows hitcount 130 on the branch for the winner, indicating the entity-name dispatch lands on 'a' and exercises the amp/apos xmlStrEqual comparisons (lines 308/310 hit only on the W side).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser never selects `case 'a':` (L T=0 F=479 at line 307); every one of its 479 invocations falls through to `default:` (L T=479 F=0 at line 317), so name[0] is never 'a' and the amp/apos comparison lines 308/310 are never reached (T=0 F=0 for the loser).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, which captures the operands of the switch dispatch on name[0] and the xmlStrEqual(name, "amp"/"apos") string comparisons, then substitutes the logged constant operand ('a', "amp", "apos") into the input bytes at the offsets where the current name appears, manufacturing entity names that route through `case 'a':`; naive has only blind havoc/token mutation and no CMP introspection, so it must stumble onto the exact first byte by chance and almost never does.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the `case 'a':` arm of a switch on name[0] in xmlGetPredefinedEntity, whose true side is globally blocked.
The dispatch is a switch on the first byte of the entity name.
Taking case 'a' leads to the amp/apos predefined-entity string comparisons.
The winner takes the 'a' case 63 times while the loser never does, falling through to default on all 479 calls.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 307  T=63 F=0  T=0 F=479  case 'a':  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser routes every invocation to the default arm, confirming name[0] is never 'a' for the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 317  T=0 F=63  T=479 F=0  default:

evidence_trail.claim: The winner hits the branch far more often than the loser (130 vs 3).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=130  loser=3

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; only blind havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6383 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 3 --seed fdfc3a57ef078849` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 17 and 18 in the 19-level ancestor chain; tool printed 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 17, 18.'

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with switch(name[0]){case 'a': ...} gated behind an entity-name string comparison would be refuted if naive resolves `case 'a':` as often as cmplog (no significant cmplog>naive divergence), showing the first-byte/string dispatch is not what I2S substitution is solving.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section shows broad whole-prefix divergence rather than a clean concentrated 'amp'/'apos' substring at a single offset, so the exact input offset where I2S writes the 'a'/amp/apos operand is inferred from source dispatch rather than read directly from the diff.
